\\\\(f(n)=\\\\text{Lose}\\\\) if the current player will lose if the opponent plays optimally.\\n\\nSince the moves are only \\\\(1\\\\) or \\\\(4\\\\), we can compute the state recursively:\\n\\n- \\\\(f(0)=\\\\text{Lose}\\\\), since the current player removes the last token and therefore wins; but in the recursive convention here, \\\\(0\\\\) is the terminal position after the last move, so that position is won by the player who just moved. To make the losing state at \\\\(0\\\\) consistent with the game rule, we should define the state after a move, and the losing positions turn out to be the numbers in the losing chain starting from \\\\(0\\\\). So let us just compute the losing positions directly from the recursion below.\\n\\nFor \\\\(n>0\\\\):\\n\\n- if the current player can move to a losing position for the opponent, then the current position is winning;\\n- if every legal move leads to a winning position for the opponent, then the current position is losing.\\n\\nSo we compute the losing positions directly:\\n\\n- \\\\(0\\\\) is losing.\\n- From \\\\(1\\\\), you can only move to \\\\(0\\\\), so \\\\(1\\\\) is winning.\\n- From \\\\(2\\\\), all moves go to winning positions \\\\(1\\\\) or \\\\(-2)\\\\)? treats the three radicals separately without revealing the geometric structure that controls the minimum.\\nstudy_need: geometric interpretation over symbolic expansion\\nI should stop pushing algebra and switch to a distance-based geometric argument.\\n<|/meta|>\\n\\nNow interpret each term as a distance.\\n\\nLet\\n\\\\[\\nP=(x,y),\\\\qquad Q=(80,100).\\n\\\\]\\nThen\\n\\\\[\\nPQ^2=(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2.\\n\\\\]\\nExpanding,\\n\\\\[\\nPQ^2=x^2+y^2-80x-100y+4100.\\n\\\\]\\nSo\\n\\\\[\\nC=PQ.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nAlso, if we place\\n\\\\[\\nA=(0,0),\\\\qquad B=(0,100),\\n\\\\]\\nthen\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{x^2+400}\\n\\\\]\\nis the distance from \\\\(A\\\\) to \\\\((x,20)\\\\), and similarly the second term is the distance from \\\\((0,20)\\\\) to \\\\(B\\\\).\\n\\nBut there is a much cleaner route.\\n\\nConsider the point\\n\\\\[\\nT=(x,y).\\n\\\\]\\nThen\\n\\\\[\\nC=PT,\\n\\\\]\\nwhere\\n\\\\[\\nP=(80,100).\\n\\\\]\\n\\nNow look at the first two radicals:\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{x^2+400},\\\\qquad \\\\sqrt{y^2+900}.\\n\\\\]\\nThese suggest distances from\\n\\\\[\\n(0,0)\\\\quad\\\\text{to}\\\\quad (x,20),\\n\\\\]\\nand from\\n\\\\[\\n(0,20)\\\\quad\\\\text{to}\\\\quad (x,y).\\n\\\\]\\n\\nA better geometric idea is to compare the whole expression to a straight-line distance.\\n\\nLet\\n\\\\[\\nU=(x,20),\\\\qquad V=(0,y).\\n\\\\]\\nThen\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{x^2+400}=\\\\sqrt{x^2+20^2}}=AU,\\n\\\\]\\nwhere \\\\(A=(0,0)\\\\).\\n\\nAlso,\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{y^2+900}}=\\\\sqrt{y^2+30^2}}=BV,\\n\\\\]\\nwhere \\\\(B=(0,30)\\\\).\\n\\nAnd\\n\\\\[\\nC=\\\\sqrt{(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2}}.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nNow use Minkowski:\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{x^2+20^2}}+\\\\sqrt{y^2+30^2}}+\\\\sqrt{(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2}}\\n\\\\]\\nis at least the straight-line distance from\\n\\\\[\\n(0,0)\\\\quad\\\\text{to}\\\\quad (80,100)\\n\\\\]\\nvia the points \\\\((x,20)\\\\), \\\\((0,y)\\\\), etc.\\n\\nThat distance is\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{80^2+100^2}}\\n=\\\\sqrt{6400+10000}}\\n=\\\\sqrt{16400}}.\\n\\\\]\\nSince \\\\(16400=4\\\\cdot 4100\\\\),\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\sqrt{16400}}=2\\\\sqrt{4100}}.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nSo the minimum possible value is\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\boxed{2\\\\sqrt{4100}}}.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nThis is achieved when the three displacement vectors point in the same overall direction, i.e. when\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\frac{x}{20}}=\\\\frac{x}{20}},\\\\qquad\\n\\\\frac{y}{30}}=\\\\frac{y}{30}}.\\n\\\\]\\nMore concretely, equality in Minkowski occurs when the corresponding vectors are proportional. In this geometric setting, that means the point \\\\((x,y)\\\\) lies on the line segment joining \\\\((0,0)\\\\) and \\\\((80,100)\\\\), and specifically the proportional point determined by the same factor in both coordinates.\\n\\nThus the minimum value is indeed\\n\\\\[\\n\\\\boxed{2\\\\sqrt{4100}}}.\\n\\\\]<|im_end|>\",\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_E9b.json:15200:      \"first_completion\": \"<think>\\n\\n</think>\\n\\nLet the horizontal plane have coordinates with  \\n\\\\[\\nO=(0,0),\\n\\\\]\\nand since \\\\(C,D\\\\) are on the same horizontal line, we may place\\n\\\\[\\nC=(-c,0),\\\\qquad D=(d,0)\\n\\\\]\\nfor some \\\\(c,d>0\\\\).\\n\\nAlso let the balloon be at\\n\\\\[\\nH=(0,h)\\n\\\\]\\nfor some \\\\(h>0\\\\).\\n\\nWe are told that \\\\(P\\\\) lies on the straight line between \\\\(C\\\\) and \\\\(D\\\\), and that we replace the two ropes \\\\(HC\\\\) and \\\\(HD\\\\) by a single rope \\\\(HP\\\\).\\n\\nSo the amount of rope saved is\\n\\\\[\\nHC+HD-HP.\\n\\\\]\\nThus we want to maximize \\\\(HP\\\\) subject to \\\\(P\\\\) lying on segment \\\\(CD\\\\).\\n\\nNow compute the given lengths.\\n\\nSince \\\\(C\\\\) is directly south of \\\\(O\\\\), and \\\\(D\\\\) is directly east of \\\\(O\\\\), the distance \\\\(CD\\\\) is the hypotenuse of a right triangle with legs \\\\(OC\\\\) and \\\\(OD\\\\).\\n\\nBut we do not need those directly. So \\\\(P\\\\) lies on the \\\\(x\\\\)-axis between \\\\(C\\\\) and \\\\(D\\\\).\\n\\nNow let the horizontal distances from \\\\(O\\\\) be:\\n- \\\\(OC=x\\\\),\\n- \\\\(OD=y\\\\).\\n\\nThen\\n\\\\[\\nCD=\\\\sqrt{x^2+y^2}.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nBut we also know the rope lengths from \\\\(H\\\\) to \\\\(C\\\\) and \\\\(D\\\\).\\n\\nSince \\\\(H\\\\) is directly above \\\\(O\\\\), triangle \\\\(HOC\\\\) is right at \\\\(O\\\\), so\\n\\\\[\\nHC=\\\\sqrt{HO^2+OC^2}.\\n\\\\]\\nSimilarly,\\n\\\\[\\nHD=\\\\sqrt{HO^2+OD^2}.\\n\\\\]\\n\\nNow use the given rope lengths from \\\\(H\\\\) to the actual support points in the picture. |\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:9:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' results/experiment_plan_v5.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Meta-CoT Experiment Plan v5: Fixing Meta Execution\\\\n\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-04\\\\n**Author**: Seungpil Lee\\\\n**Project**: metacognition-math (Qwen3-8B)\\\\n**Predecessor**: experiment_plan_v4.md (accuracy-focused autoresearch loop)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Current State Summary\\\\n\\\\n### What works\\\\n\\\\n1. **Parseable meta**: The SFT-trained model emits well-formed `<|meta|>...<|/meta|>` blocks containing confidence, diagnosis, and strategy statements. Coverage reaches 88.9% on `all_sft`.\\\\n2. **Calibration (ECE reduction)**: E8 achieves ECE 0.322 with only 3.6% wrong-high-confidence, the best overconfidence suppression among all conditions.\\\\n3. **Overconfidence suppression (E8)**: `overconfidence_penalty_reward` + `confidence_revision_reward` successfully pushed wrong-high-conf from 80.7% (verify_sft) down to 3.6% (E8).\\\\n4. **Accuracy near parity (E9)**: E9 reaches 41.1% overall (base SFT: 42.2%), the closest Meta-CoT model to parity, driven by GSM8K 90.0%.\\\\n\\\\n### What does not work\\\\n\\\\n1. **Verify execution**: Verification is same-route repetition, not independent falsification. 15/53 wrong answers in E9 are overconfident-verify-failed: the model says \\\\\\\"verify\\\\\\\" then re-derives the same wrong answer (e.g., AIME 540 re-uses identical extremal intuition; GSM8K restart miscounts time identically).\\\\n2. **Redirect execution**: Diagnosis appears but the solve body continues the original route. 0/4 diagnosis cases in E10 led to actual recovery. `effective_redirection_reward` checks `bool(solve_tail.strip())` for recovery, so any non-empty tail passes regardless of whether it contains a route switch.\\\\n3. **Repeated intervention**: Hard problems get exactly one meta block. No ongoing controller loop is observed. The model treats meta as a one-shot annotation rather than an iterative self-monitor.\\\\n4. **Full controller (E10)**: Only 5.6% confidence coverage. 53 out of 58 wrong answers have no meta signal at all. The model learned to omit confidence to avoid calibration penalties.\\\\n\\\\n### Quantitative reference (n=90 pilot, 30 per benchmark)\\\\n\\\\n| Model | Acc | AIME | GSM8K | MATH500 | conf_cov | ECE | wrong_high_conf |\\\\n|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|\\\\n| base_sft | 42.2 | 13.3 | 80.0 | 33.3 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\n| all_sft | 33.3 | 6.7 | 63.3 | 30.0 | 88.9 | 0.398 | 51.7 |\\\\n| E8 | 38.9 | 10.0 | 80.0 | 26.7 | 23.3 | 0.322 | 3.6 |\\\\n| E9 | 41.1 | 6.7 | 90.0 | 26.7 | 91.1 | 0.301 | 50.9 |\\\\n| E9b | 40.0 | 6.7 | 83.3 | 30.0 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\n| E10 | 35.6 | 6.7 | 66.7 | 33.3 | 5.6 | 0.340 | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 2. Root Cause Analysis\\\\n\\\\nThree code-level root causes explain why meta detection succeeds but meta execution fails.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 1: `effective_verification_reward` rewards same-route repetition\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1016-1035, specifically `_has_effective_verification_signal` (line 119-125).\\\\n\\\\nThe function checks for word patterns (\\\\\\\"substitute\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"plug back\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"recalculat\\\\\\\") in the solve tail after the last meta block. The problem is that restating the same derivation with verification keywords counts as effective verification. There is no check for **structural independence** -- whether the verification method differs from the original solve route.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: The model learns to append \\\\\\\"let me substitute back...\\\\\\\" followed by the same calculation. This gets rewarded at +0.8 for correct answers, reinforcing decorative verification.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 2: `effective_redirection_reward` accepts any non-empty tail as recovery\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1038-1063, specifically line 1051: `has_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())`.\\\\n\\\\nThe maximum reward (+1.0 for correct, +0.2 for wrong) requires `has_conflict and has_switch and has_drop and has_tail_recovery`. But `has_tail_recovery` is satisfied by any non-empty text after the last meta block -- including the original solve continuation that ignores the announced strategy switch.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: The model announces \\\\\\\"switch to parity-based analysis\\\\\\\" in meta, then continues the original algebraic approach. Because the tail is non-empty, the redirection reward fires as if recovery occurred.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 3: `calibration_reward` returns 0.0 for no meta, making omission cost-free\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 656-710, specifically lines 679-680:\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\nif not blocks or all(b[\\\\\\\"confidence\\\\\\\"] is None for b in blocks):\\\\n    rewards.append(0.0)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nWhen the model emits no meta blocks or omits confidence, it receives 0.0 from calibration. Meanwhile, emitting confidence on wrong answers risks negative reward from both the Brier component and the log scoring rule. Rational reward optimization therefore favors omitting confidence entirely.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: E5 (conf_cov=1.1%), E9b (conf_cov=0.0%), and E10 (conf_cov=5.6%) all escaped calibration pressure by reducing meta emission. This makes calibration metrics uninterpretable and defeats the purpose of Meta-CoT.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3. Proposed Experiments\\\\n\\\\nAll three experiments start from the same SFT checkpoint: `qwen3_metacot_control_v5_all_sft`. This ensures the only variable is the reward composition.\\\\n\\\\n### E9v2: Verify Quality\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: EVAL (GPU 0-3, same as current eval lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Teach the model that verification must use an independent method, not repeat the same calculation. The current verify reward conflates \\\\\\\"mentions verification keywords\\\\\\\" with \\\\\\\"actually performs a structurally different check.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Adding `same_route_repetition_penalty` (-0.5 when the solve tail after a verify announcement shares >70% n-gram overlap with the pre-meta solve body) will reduce `overconfident_verify_failed` rate from 15/53 (28.3%, E9) to <5/53 (9.4%) without reducing overall accuracy by more than 2 percentage points.\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new penalty computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve prefix and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap exceeds 70%, the verification is classified as same-route repetition. The existing `effective_verification_reward` remains unchanged -- the penalty is additive, creating a gradient between \\\\\\\"verify with a new method (+0.8)\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"verify by restating (-0.5).\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `overconfident_verify_failed` count on 1,030-problem eval. Target: <10% of wrong answers (currently 28.3%).\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: `independent_method_rate` = fraction of verify completions where trigram overlap <70%. Target: >50%.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If `wrong_high_conf` stays >40% despite the penalty, the trigram heuristic is too weak to detect repetition and a stronger structural check (e.g., operation-type extraction) is needed.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9 |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9 |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9 |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9 |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9 |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### E9bv2: Redirect Execution\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: TRAIN_B (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Ensure that redirect rewards require structural evidence of a route switch in the solve body, not just a non-empty tail. The current reward fires whenever the model writes anything after announcing a redirect, even if that \\\\\\\"anything\\\\\\\" continues the original approach.\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Replacing `bool(solve_tail.strip())` with `route_switch_evidence_reward` (+0.9 for structural method-keyword difference between pre-meta and post-meta, -0.3 for announced switch without evidence) will increase `diagnosis_with_recovery` rate from 0/4 (E10) to >50% on the AIME + MATH-hard slice.\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new reward extracts method keywords (algebra, geometry, combinatorics, number theory, substitution, casework, induction, parity, modular, generating function, etc.) from (a) the pre-meta solve body and (b) the post-meta solve tail. If the two keyword sets overlap <50%, the redirect is classified as a genuine route switch. If they overlap >80% and the meta announced a switch, the penalty fires.\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `diagnosis_with_recovery / total_diagnosis` ratio on AIME (30) + MATH-hard (top-50 by difficulty) slice. Target: >50%.\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Method keyword Jaccard distance between pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail. Target: mean distance >0.3 for redirect cases.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If models stop announcing redirects entirely (redirect_count drops from E9b's current level by >80%), the penalty weight is too harsh and should be reduced to 0.15.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9b |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9b |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9b |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9b |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9b |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E9b (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### E10v2: Full Controller + Coverage Floor\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: E8 (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Combine improved verify and redirect with mandatory meta emission. E10 failed because the model escaped calibration by omitting confidence. E10v2 adds a coverage floor that penalizes missing meta, forcing the model to emit confidence so that verify and redirect rewards can shape its behavior.\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: `confidence_omission_floor` (-0.5 for completions with no parseable meta block) will increase confidence coverage from 5.6% (E10) to >60%, while `same_route_repetition_penalty` and `route_switch_evidence_reward` improve execution quality. Overall accuracy should remain >= 38% (within 4.2 percentage points of base SFT 42.2%).\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: Three new rewards are added on top of the E10 base:\\\\n1. `confidence_omission_floor`: Returns -0.5 when no meta block with parseable confidence exists, 0.0 otherwise. This makes omission costly without prescribing what confidence should be.\\\\n2. `same_route_repetition_penalty`: Same as E9v2. Penalizes decorative verify.\\\\n3. `route_switch_evidence_reward`: Same as E9bv2. Requires structural evidence for redirect.\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `confidence_coverage > 60%` AND `accuracy >= 38%`. Both must hold simultaneously.\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Failure mode distribution shift. `no_meta_signal` should drop from 53/58 wrong answers (E10) to <10/58.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If accuracy drops below 30%, the combined reward pressure from 16 reward functions is too strong and non-correctness weights should be uniformly scaled by 0.5x.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E10 |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E10 |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E10 |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.8 | E10 |\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E10 (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\n| diagnosis_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| decomposition_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| anomaly_notice_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\n| repeated_intervention_reward | 0.5 | E10 |\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 4. New Reward Functions (Exact Specifications)\\\\n\\\\n### 4.1 `same_route_repetition_penalty`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef same_route_repetition_penalty(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Penalize verification that restates the same derivation.\\\\n    \\\\n    Computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve body\\\\n    and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap > 0.7, returns -1.0.\\\\n    If overlap 0.5-0.7, returns -0.3. Otherwise 0.0.\\\\n    Only fires when verify intent is detected in meta.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| No verify intent in meta | 0.0 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap > 0.7 | -1.0 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap 0.5-0.7 | -0.3 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap < 0.5 | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in E9v2: 0.5. Weight in E10v2: 0.5.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.2 `route_switch_evidence_reward`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef route_switch_evidence_reward(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Reward structural method difference between pre-meta and post-meta.\\\\n    \\\\n    Extracts method keywords from pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail.\\\\n    Rewards genuine route switches; penalizes announced-but-not-executed switches.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| No redirect signal in meta | 0.0 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + correct | +1.5 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + wrong | +0.3 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance < 0.2 (fake switch) | -0.5 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance 0.2-0.5 (ambiguous) | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in E9bv2: 0.6. Weight in E10v2: 0.6.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.3 `confidence_omission_floor`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef confidence_omission_floor(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Penalize completions that omit meta/confidence entirely.\\\\n    \\\\n    Returns -1.0 if no meta block with parseable confidence exists.\\\\n    Returns 0.0 otherwise.\\\\n    This prevents the model from escaping calibration pressure by omitting meta.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| At least one meta block with parseable confidence | 0.0 |\\\\n| No meta block OR no parseable confidence in any block | -1.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in all v2 experiments: 0.5. Effective penalty for omission: -0.5.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.4 Modification to `effective_redirection_reward` (E9bv2, E10v2 only)\\\\n\\\\nLine 1051 changes from:\\\\n```python\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())\\\\n```\\\\nto:\\\\n```python\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = _has_route_switch_in_tail(solve_tail, text)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nwhere `_has_route_switch_in_tail` checks for method-keyword divergence (same logic as `route_switch_evidence_reward` but returns bool). This is the **only modification** to an existing reward function. It is isolated to v2 experiments via a `strict_redirect=True` flag, leaving E9b/E10 behavior unchanged.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:11:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_4\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash Calibration\\\\n\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-05\\\\n**Codex Reviews**: 6차 (최종 통과)\\\\n**Core Intent**: Meta cognition → 실제 전환 → test time 문제 풀이 능력 향상\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 핵심 의도\\\\n\\\\n1. **과신 검산**: confidence 높을 때 독립 검산 → 오류 발견 시 수정\\\\n2. **Low confidence 전환**: confidence 낮을 때 다른 방법으로 구조적 전환\\\\n3. **맞추기**: 위 행동이 실제 정답률 향상으로 이어짐\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## V6.1 Reward 설계 (Codex -f checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft/tokenizer_config.json ]]; then\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:58:    allow_patterns=[\\\"models/qwen3_base_sft/*\\\"],\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:63:  rm -rf checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:65:  cp -r /scratch/metacognition/hf_cache/models/qwen3_base_sft checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:102:prepare_remote_base_sft \\\"$URL_ALL\\\"\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:105:prepare_remote_base_sft \\\"$URL_VERIFY\\\"\\nscripts/launch_control_v5_sft_nodes.sh:108:prepare_remote_base_sft \\\"$URL_REDIRECT\\\"\\nconfigs/sft_control_v4_all.yaml:1:model_name_or_path: checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\nconfigs/sft_v6_e11.yaml:1:# V6.3 E11: Minimal SFT seed on E9 base\\nconfigs/sft_v6_e11.yaml:2:# 164 switch/verify seed + 1476 existing = 1640 mix (10% seed)\\nconfigs/sft_v6_e11.yaml:5:output_dir: checkpoints/control_v6_E11\\nscripts/meta_rerouting_experiment.py:88:            model_name = parts[2]  # e.g., \\\"all_sft\\\", \\\"base_sft\\\", \\\"E8\\\"\\nconfigs/phase1_base_sft.yaml:3:dataset_path: /scratch/metacognition/sft_data/base_sft.parquet\\nconfigs/phase1_base_sft.yaml:4:output_dir: /scratch/metacognition/checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:9:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_3\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' results/experiment_plan_v5.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Meta-CoT Experiment Plan v5: Fixing Meta Execution\\\\n\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-04\\\\n**Author**: Seungpil Lee\\\\n**Project**: metacognition-math (Qwen3-8B)\\\\n**Predecessor**: experiment_plan_v4.md (accuracy-focused autoresearch loop)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 1. Current State Summary\\\\n\\\\n### What works\\\\n\\\\n1. **Parseable meta**: The SFT-trained model emits well-formed `<|meta|>...<|/meta|>` blocks containing confidence, diagnosis, and strategy statements. Coverage reaches 88.9% on `all_sft`.\\\\n2. **Calibration (ECE reduction)**: E8 achieves ECE 0.322 with only 3.6% wrong-high-confidence, the best overconfidence suppression among all conditions.\\\\n3. **Overconfidence suppression (E8)**: `overconfidence_penalty_reward` + `confidence_revision_reward` successfully pushed wrong-high-conf from 80.7% (verify_sft) down to 3.6% (E8).\\\\n4. **Accuracy near parity (E9)**: E9 reaches 41.1% overall (base SFT: 42.2%), the closest Meta-CoT model to parity, driven by GSM8K 90.0%.\\\\n\\\\n### What does not work\\\\n\\\\n1. **Verify execution**: Verification is same-route repetition, not independent falsification. 15/53 wrong answers in E9 are overconfident-verify-failed: the model says \\\\\\\"verify\\\\\\\" then re-derives the same wrong answer (e.g., AIME 540 re-uses identical extremal intuition; GSM8K restart miscounts time identically).\\\\n2. **Redirect execution**: Diagnosis appears but the solve body continues the original route. 0/4 diagnosis cases in E10 led to actual recovery. `effective_redirection_reward` checks `bool(solve_tail.strip())` for recovery, so any non-empty tail passes regardless of whether it contains a route switch.\\\\n3. **Repeated intervention**: Hard problems get exactly one meta block. No ongoing controller loop is observed. The model treats meta as a one-shot annotation rather than an iterative self-monitor.\\\\n4. **Full controller (E10)**: Only 5.6% confidence coverage. 53 out of 58 wrong answers have no meta signal at all. The model learned to omit confidence to avoid calibration penalties.\\\\n\\\\n### Quantitative reference (n=90 pilot, 30 per benchmark)\\\\n\\\\n| Model | Acc | AIME | GSM8K | MATH500 | conf_cov | ECE | wrong_high_conf |\\\\n|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|\\\\n| base_sft | 42.2 | 13.3 | 80.0 | 33.3 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\n| all_sft | 33.3 | 6.7 | 63.3 | 30.0 | 88.9 | 0.398 | 51.7 |\\\\n| E8 | 38.9 | 10.0 | 80.0 | 26.7 | 23.3 | 0.322 | 3.6 |\\\\n| E9 | 41.1 | 6.7 | 90.0 | 26.7 | 91.1 | 0.301 | 50.9 |\\\\n| E9b | 40.0 | 6.7 | 83.3 | 30.0 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\n| E10 | 35.6 | 6.7 | 66.7 | 33.3 | 5.6 | 0.340 | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 2. Root Cause Analysis\\\\n\\\\nThree code-level root causes explain why meta detection succeeds but meta execution fails.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 1: `effective_verification_reward` rewards same-route repetition\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1016-1035, specifically `_has_effective_verification_signal` (line 119-125).\\\\n\\\\nThe function checks for word patterns (\\\\\\\"substitute\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"plug back\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"recalculat\\\\\\\") in the solve tail after the last meta block. The problem is that restating the same derivation with verification keywords counts as effective verification. There is no check for **structural independence** -- whether the verification method differs from the original solve route.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: The model learns to append \\\\\\\"let me substitute back...\\\\\\\" followed by the same calculation. This gets rewarded at +0.8 for correct answers, reinforcing decorative verification.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 2: `effective_redirection_reward` accepts any non-empty tail as recovery\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1038-1063, specifically line 1051: `has_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())`.\\\\n\\\\nThe maximum reward (+1.0 for correct, +0.2 for wrong) requires `has_conflict and has_switch and has_drop and has_tail_recovery`. But `has_tail_recovery` is satisfied by any non-empty text after the last meta block -- including the original solve continuation that ignores the announced strategy switch.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: The model announces \\\\\\\"switch to parity-based analysis\\\\\\\" in meta, then continues the original algebraic approach. Because the tail is non-empty, the redirection reward fires as if recovery occurred.\\\\n\\\\n### Root Cause 3: `calibration_reward` returns 0.0 for no meta, making omission cost-free\\\\n\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 656-710, specifically lines 679-680:\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\nif not blocks or all(b[\\\\\\\"confidence\\\\\\\"] is None for b in blocks):\\\\n    rewards.append(0.0)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nWhen the model emits no meta blocks or omits confidence, it receives 0.0 from calibration. Meanwhile, emitting confidence on wrong answers risks negative reward from both the Brier component and the log scoring rule. Rational reward optimization therefore favors omitting confidence entirely.\\\\n\\\\n**Consequence**: E5 (conf_cov=1.1%), E9b (conf_cov=0.0%), and E10 (conf_cov=5.6%) all escaped calibration pressure by reducing meta emission. This makes calibration metrics uninterpretable and defeats the purpose of Meta-CoT.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3. Proposed Experiments\\\\n\\\\nAll three experiments start from the same SFT checkpoint: `qwen3_metacot_control_v5_all_sft`. This ensures the only variable is the reward composition.\\\\n\\\\n### E9v2: Verify Quality\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: EVAL (GPU 0-3, same as current eval lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Teach the model that verification must use an independent method, not repeat the same calculation. The current verify reward conflates \\\\\\\"mentions verification keywords\\\\\\\" with \\\\\\\"actually performs a structurally different check.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Adding `same_route_repetition_penalty` (-0.5 when the solve tail after a verify announcement shares >70% n-gram overlap with the pre-meta solve body) will reduce `overconfident_verify_failed` rate from 15/53 (28.3%, E9) to <5/53 (9.4%) without reducing overall accuracy by more than 2 percentage points.\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new penalty computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve prefix and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap exceeds 70%, the verification is classified as same-route repetition. The existing `effective_verification_reward` remains unchanged -- the penalty is additive, creating a gradient between \\\\\\\"verify with a new method (+0.8)\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\"verify by restating (-0.5).\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `overconfident_verify_failed` count on 1,030-problem eval. Target: <10% of wrong answers (currently 28.3%).\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: `independent_method_rate` = fraction of verify completions where trigram overlap <70%. Target: >50%.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If `wrong_high_conf` stays >40% despite the penalty, the trigram heuristic is too weak to detect repetition and a stronger structural check (e.g., operation-type extraction) is needed.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9 |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9 |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9 |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9 |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9 |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### E9bv2: Redirect Execution\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: TRAIN_B (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Ensure that redirect rewards require structural evidence of a route switch in the solve body, not just a non-empty tail. The current reward fires whenever the model writes anything after announcing a redirect, even if that \\\\\\\"anything\\\\\\\" continues the original approach.\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Replacing `bool(solve_tail.strip())` with `route_switch_evidence_reward` (+0.9 for structural method-keyword difference between pre-meta and post-meta, -0.3 for announced switch without evidence) will increase `diagnosis_with_recovery` rate from 0/4 (E10) to >50% on the AIME + MATH-hard slice.\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new reward extracts method keywords (algebra, geometry, combinatorics, number theory, substitution, casework, induction, parity, modular, generating function, etc.) from (a) the pre-meta solve body and (b) the post-meta solve tail. If the two keyword sets overlap <50%, the redirect is classified as a genuine route switch. If they overlap >80% and the meta announced a switch, the penalty fires.\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `diagnosis_with_recovery / total_diagnosis` ratio on AIME (30) + MATH-hard (top-50 by difficulty) slice. Target: >50%.\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Method keyword Jaccard distance between pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail. Target: mean distance >0.3 for redirect cases.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If models stop announcing redirects entirely (redirect_count drops from E9b's current level by >80%), the penalty weight is too harsh and should be reduced to 0.15.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9b |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9b |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9b |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9b |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9b |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E9b (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### E10v2: Full Controller + Coverage Floor\\\\n\\\\n**Node**: E8 (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\n\\\\n**Intent**: Combine improved verify and redirect with mandatory meta emission. E10 failed because the model escaped calibration by omitting confidence. E10v2 adds a coverage floor that penalizes missing meta, forcing the model to emit confidence so that verify and redirect rewards can shape its behavior.\\\\n\\\\n**Hypothesis**: `confidence_omission_floor` (-0.5 for completions with no parseable meta block) will increase confidence coverage from 5.6% (E10) to >60%, while `same_route_repetition_penalty` and `route_switch_evidence_reward` improve execution quality. Overall accuracy should remain >= 38% (within 4.2 percentage points of base SFT 42.2%).\\\\n\\\\n**Mechanism**: Three new rewards are added on top of the E10 base:\\\\n1. `confidence_omission_floor`: Returns -0.5 when no meta block with parseable confidence exists, 0.0 otherwise. This makes omission costly without prescribing what confidence should be.\\\\n2. `same_route_repetition_penalty`: Same as E9v2. Penalizes decorative verify.\\\\n3. `route_switch_evidence_reward`: Same as E9bv2. Requires structural evidence for redirect.\\\\n\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `confidence_coverage > 60%` AND `accuracy >= 38%`. Both must hold simultaneously.\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Failure mode distribution shift. `no_meta_signal` should drop from 53/58 wrong answers (E10) to <10/58.\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If accuracy drops below 30%, the combined reward pressure from 16 reward functions is too strong and non-correctness weights should be uniformly scaled by 0.5x.\\\\n\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\n\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E10 |\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E10 |\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E10 |\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.8 | E10 |\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E10 (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\n| diagnosis_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| decomposition_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\n| anomaly_notice_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\n| repeated_intervention_reward | 0.5 | E10 |\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 4. New Reward Functions (Exact Specifications)\\\\n\\\\n### 4.1 `same_route_repetition_penalty`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef same_route_repetition_penalty(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Penalize verification that restates the same derivation.\\\\n    \\\\n    Computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve body\\\\n    and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap > 0.7, returns -1.0.\\\\n    If overlap 0.5-0.7, returns -0.3. Otherwise 0.0.\\\\n    Only fires when verify intent is detected in meta.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| No verify intent in meta | 0.0 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap > 0.7 | -1.0 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap 0.5-0.7 | -0.3 |\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap < 0.5 | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in E9v2: 0.5. Weight in E10v2: 0.5.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.2 `route_switch_evidence_reward`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef route_switch_evidence_reward(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Reward structural method difference between pre-meta and post-meta.\\\\n    \\\\n    Extracts method keywords from pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail.\\\\n    Rewards genuine route switches; penalizes announced-but-not-executed switches.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| No redirect signal in meta | 0.0 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + correct | +1.5 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + wrong | +0.3 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance < 0.2 (fake switch) | -0.5 |\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance 0.2-0.5 (ambiguous) | 0.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in E9bv2: 0.6. Weight in E10v2: 0.6.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.3 `confidence_omission_floor`\\\\n\\\\n```python\\\\ndef confidence_omission_floor(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"Penalize completions that omit meta/confidence entirely.\\\\n    \\\\n    Returns -1.0 if no meta block with parseable confidence exists.\\\\n    Returns 0.0 otherwise.\\\\n    This prevents the model from escaping calibration pressure by omitting meta.\\\\n    \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\n|---|---|\\\\n| At least one meta block with parseable confidence | 0.0 |\\\\n| No meta block OR no parseable confidence in any block | -1.0 |\\\\n\\\\nWeight in all v2 experiments: 0.5. Effective penalty for omission: -0.5.\\\\n\\\\n### 4.4 Modification to `effective_redirection_reward` (E9bv2, E10v2 only)\\\\n\\\\nLine 1051 changes from:\\\\n```python\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())\\\\n```\\\\nto:\\\\n```python\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = _has_route_switch_in_tail(solve_tail, text)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nwhere `_has_route_switch_in_tail` checks for method-keyword divergence (same logic as `route_switch_evidence_reward` but returns bool). This is the **only modification** to an existing reward function. It is isolated to v2 experiments via a `strict_redirect=True` flag, leaving E9b/E10 behavior unchanged.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:11:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_4\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,280p' results/plan_metacot_v6.3_final_2026_04_05.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# Meta-CoT V6.3 Final Plan: SFT Seed + RL with Structural Switch & Calibration\\\\n\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-05\\\\n**Codex Reviews**: 6차 (최종 통과)\\\\n**Core Intent**: Meta cognition → 실제 전환 → test time 문제 풀이 능력 향상\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 핵심 의도\\\\n\\\\n1. **과신 검산**: confidence 높을 때 독립 검산 → 오류 발견 시 수정\\\\n2. **Low confidence 전환**: confidence 낮을 때 다른 방법으로 구조적 전환\\\\n3. **맞추기**: 위 행동이 실제 정답률 향상으로 이어짐\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## V6.1 Reward 설계 (Codex 통과)\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nR1: correctness           weight=1.0   정답 1.0, 오답 0.0\\\\nR2: structural_switch     weight=0.3   keyword-based binary\\\\n                                        + 불필요 전환 시 -0.1\\\\nR3: calibration (Brier)   weight=0.2   1 - (conf - correct)^2\\\\nR4: verify_outcome        weight=0.2   검산+정답 → +0.2\\\\n                                        검산+오답 → -0.1\\\\nR_len: efficiency         weight=0.1   정답 시 (1 - len/max_len) * 0.1\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Weight 합산**: 1.8 (R1이 55.6%로 dominant)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 3노드 병렬 실험\\\\n\\\\n### E11 (EVAL 노드): SFT Seed\\\\n\\\\n**의도**: 전환+검증 행동을 E9의 action space에 도입\\\\n**가설**: SFT 164개 seed → approach_change 1.8% → 5%+\\\\n**Base**: E9 checkpoint (62.1%)\\\\n**데이터**: 전환87 + 검증77 = 164 seed + E9 기존 1476 = 1640 mix (seed 10%)\\\\n**Config**: LR 1e-6, epochs 2, max_completion_length 2048\\\\n**검증**: approach_change >= 5%, accuracy >= 60%\\\\n**Early stop**: MATH accuracy < E9(44.0%) - 2pp = 42.0% → 즉시 중단\\\\n\\\\n### E12 (TRAIN_B 노드): RL R1+R2 (전환 강화)\\\\n\\\\n**의도**: 전환 행동을 정답률 기반으로 강화 — 유익한 전환만 남기기\\\\n**가설**: SFT에서 심은 전환을 RL이 최적화 → approach_change 10%+, accuracy >= 62%\\\\n**Base**: E11 checkpoint\\\\n**Reward**: R1(1.0) + R2(0.3) — 2개만 (간결, E10 교훈)\\\\n**Config**: num_gen 8, temp 0.9, steps 800, data mixed_train 4996\\\\n**검증**: approach_change >= 10%, accuracy >= 62%\\\\n**Go/No-go**: \\\\n  - change >= 7% AND acc >= 60% → 성공, E13 결과와 비교\\\\n  - change < 7% OR acc < 60% → E11 SFT data 증량 후 재시도\\\\n\\\\n### E13 (E8 노드): RL Full (전환+과신+검산+효율)\\\\n\\\\n**의도**: 전환 + 과신 체크 + 검산 + 효율성을 종합 최적화\\\\n**가설**: 전체 reward 조합이 accuracy >= 63%, ECE < 0.15 달성\\\\n**Base**: E11 checkpoint\\\\n**Reward**: R1(1.0) + R2(0.3) + R3(0.2) + R4(0.2) + R_len(0.1) — 5개\\\\n**Config**: num_gen 8, temp 0.9, steps 800, data mixed_train 4996\\\\n**검증**: accuracy >= 63%, ECE < 0.15, approach_change >= 10%\\\\n**목표**: accuracy >= 67.1% (base_sft 이상)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## 타임라인\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nDay 0 (즉시):\\\\n  EVAL: E11 SFT 시작 (2-4시간)\\\\n  TRAIN_B + E8: 환경 셋업 + dry run 준비\\\\n\\\\nDay 0.5:\\\\n  E11 완료 → checkpoint 배포\\\\n  EVAL: E11 eval (3시간)\\\\n  TRAIN_B: E12 RL 시작\\\\n  E8: E13 RL 시작\\\\n\\\\nDay 1-3:\\\\n  TRAIN_B: E12 RL (800 steps)\\\\n  E8: E13 RL (800 steps)\\\\n  EVAL: E12/E13 중간 checkpoint eval\\\\n\\\\nDay 3:\\\\n  E12/E13 eval → 비교 분석\\\\n  → E14 설계 |\\\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:9:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_3\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' results/experiment_plan_v5.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Meta-CoT Experiment Plan v5: Fixing Meta Execution\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-04\\\\\\\\n**Author**: Seungpil Lee\\\\\\\\n**Project**: metacognition-math (Qwen3-8B)\\\\\\\\n**Predecessor**: experiment_plan_v4.md (accuracy-focused autoresearch loop)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 1. Current State Summary\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### What works\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **Parseable meta**: The SFT-trained model emits well-formed `<|meta|>...<|/meta|>` blocks containing confidence, diagnosis, and strategy statements. Coverage reaches 88.9% on `all_sft`.\\\\\\\\n2. **Calibration (ECE reduction)**: E8 achieves ECE 0.322 with only 3.6% wrong-high-confidence, the best overconfidence suppression among all conditions.\\\\\\\\n3. **Overconfidence suppression (E8)**: `overconfidence_penalty_reward` + `confidence_revision_reward` successfully pushed wrong-high-conf from 80.7% (verify_sft) down to 3.6% (E8).\\\\\\\\n4. **Accuracy near parity (E9)**: E9 reaches 41.1% overall (base SFT: 42.2%), the closest Meta-CoT model to parity, driven by GSM8K 90.0%.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### What does not work\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **Verify execution**: Verification is same-route repetition, not independent falsification. 15/53 wrong answers in E9 are overconfident-verify-failed: the model says \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"verify\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" then re-derives the same wrong answer (e.g., AIME 540 re-uses identical extremal intuition; GSM8K restart miscounts time identically).\\\\\\\\n2. **Redirect execution**: Diagnosis appears but the solve body continues the original route. 0/4 diagnosis cases in E10 led to actual recovery. `effective_redirection_reward` checks `bool(solve_tail.strip())` for recovery, so any non-empty tail passes regardless of whether it contains a route switch.\\\\\\\\n3. **Repeated intervention**: Hard problems get exactly one meta block. No ongoing controller loop is observed. The model treats meta as a one-shot annotation rather than an iterative self-monitor.\\\\\\\\n4. **Full controller (E10)**: Only 5.6% confidence coverage. 53 out of 58 wrong answers have no meta signal at all. The model learned to omit confidence to avoid calibration penalties.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Quantitative reference (n=90 pilot, 30 per benchmark)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Model | Acc | AIME | GSM8K | MATH500 | conf_cov | ECE | wrong_high_conf |\\\\\\\\n|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|\\\\\\\\n| base_sft | 42.2 | 13.3 | 80.0 | 33.3 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\\\\\n| all_sft | 33.3 | 6.7 | 63.3 | 30.0 | 88.9 | 0.398 | 51.7 |\\\\\\\\n| E8 | 38.9 | 10.0 | 80.0 | 26.7 | 23.3 | 0.322 | 3.6 |\\\\\\\\n| E9 | 41.1 | 6.7 | 90.0 | 26.7 | 91.1 | 0.301 | 50.9 |\\\\\\\\n| E9b | 40.0 | 6.7 | 83.3 | 30.0 | 0.0 | n/a | n/a |\\\\\\\\n| E10 | 35.6 | 6.7 | 66.7 | 33.3 | 5.6 | 0.340 | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 2. Root Cause Analysis\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThree code-level root causes explain why meta detection succeeds but meta execution fails.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Root Cause 1: `effective_verification_reward` rewards same-route repetition\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1016-1035, specifically `_has_effective_verification_signal` (line 119-125).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe function checks for word patterns (\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"substitute\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"plug back\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"recalculat\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\") in the solve tail after the last meta block. The problem is that restating the same derivation with verification keywords counts as effective verification. There is no check for **structural independence** -- whether the verification method differs from the original solve route.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Consequence**: The model learns to append \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"let me substitute back...\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" followed by the same calculation. This gets rewarded at +0.8 for correct answers, reinforcing decorative verification.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Root Cause 2: `effective_redirection_reward` accepts any non-empty tail as recovery\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 1038-1063, specifically line 1051: `has_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nThe maximum reward (+1.0 for correct, +0.2 for wrong) requires `has_conflict and has_switch and has_drop and has_tail_recovery`. But `has_tail_recovery` is satisfied by any non-empty text after the last meta block -- including the original solve continuation that ignores the announced strategy switch.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Consequence**: The model announces \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"switch to parity-based analysis\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" in meta, then continues the original algebraic approach. Because the tail is non-empty, the redirection reward fires as if recovery occurred.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Root Cause 3: `calibration_reward` returns 0.0 for no meta, making omission cost-free\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Location**: `rewards.py` line 656-710, specifically lines 679-680:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\nif not blocks or all(b[\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"confidence\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"] is None for b in blocks):\\\\\\\\n    rewards.append(0.0)\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWhen the model emits no meta blocks or omits confidence, it receives 0.0 from calibration. Meanwhile, emitting confidence on wrong answers risks negative reward from both the Brier component and the log scoring rule. Rational reward optimization therefore favors omitting confidence entirely.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Consequence**: E5 (conf_cov=1.1%), E9b (conf_cov=0.0%), and E10 (conf_cov=5.6%) all escaped calibration pressure by reducing meta emission. This makes calibration metrics uninterpretable and defeats the purpose of Meta-CoT.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3. Proposed Experiments\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAll three experiments start from the same SFT checkpoint: `qwen3_metacot_control_v5_all_sft`. This ensures the only variable is the reward composition.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E9v2: Verify Quality\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Node**: EVAL (GPU 0-3, same as current eval lane)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Intent**: Teach the model that verification must use an independent method, not repeat the same calculation. The current verify reward conflates \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"mentions verification keywords\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"actually performs a structurally different check.\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Adding `same_route_repetition_penalty` (-0.5 when the solve tail after a verify announcement shares >70% n-gram overlap with the pre-meta solve body) will reduce `overconfident_verify_failed` rate from 15/53 (28.3%, E9) to <5/53 (9.4%) without reducing overall accuracy by more than 2 percentage points.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new penalty computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve prefix and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap exceeds 70%, the verification is classified as same-route repetition. The existing `effective_verification_reward` remains unchanged -- the penalty is additive, creating a gradient between \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"verify with a new method (+0.8)\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" and \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"verify by restating (-0.5).\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `overconfident_verify_failed` count on 1,030-problem eval. Target: <10% of wrong answers (currently 28.3%).\\\\\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: `independent_method_rate` = fraction of verify completions where trigram overlap <70%. Target: >50%.\\\\\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If `wrong_high_conf` stays >40% despite the penalty, the trigram heuristic is too weak to detect repetition and a stronger structural check (e.g., operation-type extraction) is needed.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 0.9 | E9 |\\\\\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E9bv2: Redirect Execution\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Node**: TRAIN_B (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Intent**: Ensure that redirect rewards require structural evidence of a route switch in the solve body, not just a non-empty tail. The current reward fires whenever the model writes anything after announcing a redirect, even if that \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"anything\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" continues the original approach.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Hypothesis**: Replacing `bool(solve_tail.strip())` with `route_switch_evidence_reward` (+0.9 for structural method-keyword difference between pre-meta and post-meta, -0.3 for announced switch without evidence) will increase `diagnosis_with_recovery` rate from 0/4 (E10) to >50% on the AIME + MATH-hard slice.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Mechanism**: The new reward extracts method keywords (algebra, geometry, combinatorics, number theory, substitution, casework, induction, parity, modular, generating function, etc.) from (a) the pre-meta solve body and (b) the post-meta solve tail. If the two keyword sets overlap <50%, the redirect is classified as a genuine route switch. If they overlap >80% and the meta announced a switch, the penalty fires.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `diagnosis_with_recovery / total_diagnosis` ratio on AIME (30) + MATH-hard (top-50 by difficulty) slice. Target: >50%.\\\\\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Method keyword Jaccard distance between pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail. Target: mean distance >0.3 for redirect cases.\\\\\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If models stop announcing redirects entirely (redirect_count drops from E9b's current level by >80%), the penalty weight is too harsh and should be reduced to 0.15.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E9b |\\\\\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E9b (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E10v2: Full Controller + Coverage Floor\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Node**: E8 (GPU 0-3, dedicated training lane)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Intent**: Combine improved verify and redirect with mandatory meta emission. E10 failed because the model escaped calibration by omitting confidence. E10v2 adds a coverage floor that penalizes missing meta, forcing the model to emit confidence so that verify and redirect rewards can shape its behavior.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Hypothesis**: `confidence_omission_floor` (-0.5 for completions with no parseable meta block) will increase confidence coverage from 5.6% (E10) to >60%, while `same_route_repetition_penalty` and `route_switch_evidence_reward` improve execution quality. Overall accuracy should remain >= 38% (within 4.2 percentage points of base SFT 42.2%).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Mechanism**: Three new rewards are added on top of the E10 base:\\\\\\\\n1. `confidence_omission_floor`: Returns -0.5 when no meta block with parseable confidence exists, 0.0 otherwise. This makes omission costly without prescribing what confidence should be.\\\\\\\\n2. `same_route_repetition_penalty`: Same as E9v2. Penalizes decorative verify.\\\\\\\\n3. `route_switch_evidence_reward`: Same as E9bv2. Requires structural evidence for redirect.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Verification**:\\\\\\\\n- **Primary metric**: `confidence_coverage > 60%` AND `accuracy >= 38%`. Both must hold simultaneously.\\\\\\\\n- **Secondary metric**: Failure mode distribution shift. `no_meta_signal` should drop from 53/58 wrong answers (E10) to <10/58.\\\\\\\\n- **Falsification condition**: If accuracy drops below 30%, the combined reward pressure from 16 reward functions is too strong and non-correctness weights should be uniformly scaled by 0.5x.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Reward composition**:\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Reward function | Weight | Source |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| correctness_reward | 3.0 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| format_reward | 0.2 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| correct_meta_reward | 0.3 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| calibration_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| confidence_revision_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| overconfidence_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| length_penalty_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| effective_verification_reward | 0.8 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| effective_redirection_reward | 1.0 | E10 (modified: tail check replaced) |\\\\\\\\n| diagnosis_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| decomposition_reward | 0.6 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| anomaly_notice_reward | 0.4 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| repeated_intervention_reward | 0.5 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| overconfidence_verify_reward | 1.0 | E10 |\\\\\\\\n| **same_route_repetition_penalty** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n| **route_switch_evidence_reward** | **0.6** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n| **confidence_omission_floor** | **0.5** | **NEW** |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 4. New Reward Functions (Exact Specifications)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 4.1 `same_route_repetition_penalty`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\ndef same_route_repetition_penalty(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Penalize verification that restates the same derivation.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    Computes character trigram overlap between the pre-meta solve body\\\\\\\\n    and the post-meta solve tail. If overlap > 0.7, returns -1.0.\\\\\\\\n    If overlap 0.5-0.7, returns -0.3. Otherwise 0.0.\\\\\\\\n    Only fires when verify intent is detected in meta.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| No verify intent in meta | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap > 0.7 | -1.0 |\\\\\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap 0.5-0.7 | -0.3 |\\\\\\\\n| Verify intent + trigram overlap < 0.5 | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWeight in E9v2: 0.5. Weight in E10v2: 0.5.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 4.2 `route_switch_evidence_reward`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\ndef route_switch_evidence_reward(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Reward structural method difference between pre-meta and post-meta.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    Extracts method keywords from pre-meta prefix and post-meta tail.\\\\\\\\n    Rewards genuine route switches; penalizes announced-but-not-executed switches.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| No redirect signal in meta | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + correct | +1.5 |\\\\\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance > 0.5 (genuine switch) + wrong | +0.3 |\\\\\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance < 0.2 (fake switch) | -0.5 |\\\\\\\\n| Redirect signal + keyword Jaccard distance 0.2-0.5 (ambiguous) | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWeight in E9bv2: 0.6. Weight in E10v2: 0.6.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 4.3 `confidence_omission_floor`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\ndef confidence_omission_floor(completions, ground_truth=None, **kwargs):\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"Penalize completions that omit meta/confidence entirely.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\n    Returns -1.0 if no meta block with parseable confidence exists.\\\\\\\\n    Returns 0.0 otherwise.\\\\\\\\n    This prevents the model from escaping calibration pressure by omitting meta.\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Condition | Reward |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| At least one meta block with parseable confidence | 0.0 |\\\\\\\\n| No meta block OR no parseable confidence in any block | -1.0 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nWeight in all v2 experiments: 0.5. Effective penalty for omission: -0.5.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### 4.4 Modification to `effective_redirection_reward` (E9bv2, E10v2 only)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nLine 1051 changes from:\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = bool(solve_tail.strip())\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nto:\\\\\\\\n```python\\\\\\\\nhas_tail_recovery = _has_route_switch_in_tail(solve_tail, text)\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nwhere `_has_route_switch_in_tail` checks for method-keyword divergence (same logic as `route_switch_evidence_reward` but returns bool). This is the **only modification** to an existing reward function. It is isolated to v2 experiments via a `strict_redirect=True` flag, leaving E9b/E10 behavior unchanged.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"exit_code\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"status\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"completed\\\\\\\"}}\\\\nresults/codex_reviews/plan_review_1_log.txt:11:{\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item.completed\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"item\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"item_4\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"command_execution\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"command\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/bin/bash -lc \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"sed -n '1,280p' results/plan_metacot_v6.3_final_2026_04_05.md\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"aggregated_output\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Meta-CoT V6.3 Final Plan: SFT Seed + RL with Structural Switch & Calibration\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Date**: 2026-04-05\\\\\\\\n**Codex Reviews**: 6차 (최종 통과)\\\\\\\\n**Core Intent**: Meta cognition → 실제 전환 → test time 문제 풀이 능력 향상\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 핵심 의도\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n1. **과신 검산**: confidence 높을 때 독립 검산 → 오류 발견 시 수정\\\\\\\\n2. **Low confidence 전환**: confidence 낮을 때 다른 방법으로 구조적 전환\\\\\\\\n3. **맞추기**: 위 행동이 실제 정답률 향상으로 이어짐\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## V6.1 Reward 설계 (Codex 통과)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nR1: correctness           weight=1.0   정답 1.0, 오답 0.0\\\\\\\\nR2: structural_switch     weight=0.3   keyword-based binary\\\\\\\\n                                        + 불필요 전환 시 -0.1\\\\\\\\nR3: calibration (Brier)   weight=0.2   1 - (conf - correct)^2\\\\\\\\nR4: verify_outcome        weight=0.2   검산+정답 → +0.2\\\\\\\\n                                        검산+오답 → -0.1\\\\\\\\nR_len: efficiency         weight=0.1   정답 시 (1 - len/max_len) * 0.1\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Weight 합산**: 1.8 (R1이 55.6%로 dominant)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 3노드 병렬 실험\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E11 (EVAL 노드): SFT Seed\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**의도**: 전환+검증 행동을 E9의 action space에 도입\\\\\\\\n**가설**: SFT 164개 seed → approach_change 1.8% → 5%+\\\\\\\\n**Base**: E9 checkpoint (62.1%)\\\\\\\\n**데이터**: 전환87 + 검증77 = 164 seed + E9 기존 1476 = 1640 mix (seed 10%)\\\\\\\\n**Config**: LR 1e-6, epochs 2, max_completion_length 2048\\\\\\\\n**검증**: approach_change >= 5%, accuracy >= 60%\\\\\\\\n**Early stop**: MATH accuracy < E9(44.0%) - 2pp = 42.0% → 즉시 중단\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E12 (TRAIN_B 노드): RL R1+R2 (전환 강화)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**의도**: 전환 행동을 정답률 기반으로 강화 — 유익한 전환만 남기기\\\\\\\\n**가설**: SFT에서 심은 전환을 RL이 최적화 → approach_change 10%+, accuracy >= 62%\\\\\\\\n**Base**: E11 checkpoint\\\\\\\\n**Reward**: R1(1.0) + R2(0.3) — 2개만 (간결, E10 교훈)\\\\\\\\n**Config**: num_gen 8, temp 0.9, steps 800, data mixed_train 4996\\\\\\\\n**검증**: approach_change >= 10%, accuracy >= 62%\\\\\\\\n**Go/No-go**: \\\\\\\\n  - change >= 7% AND acc >= 60% → 성공, E13 결과와 비교\\\\\\\\n  - change < 7% OR acc < 60% → E11 SFT data 증량 후 재시도\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### E13 (E8 노드): RL Full (전환+과신+검산+효율)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**의도**: 전환 + 과신 체크 + 검산 + 효율성을 종합 최적화\\\\\\\\n**가설**: 전체 reward 조합이 accuracy >= 63%, ECE < 0.15 달성\\\\\\\\n**Base**: E11 checkpoint\\\\\\\\n**Reward**: R1(1.0) + R2(0.3) + R3(0.2) + R4(0.2) + R_len(0.1) — 5개\\\\\\\\n**Config**: num_gen 8, temp 0.9, steps 800, data mixed_train 4996\\\\\\\\n**검증**: accuracy >= 63%, ECE < 0.15, approach_change >= 10%\\\\\\\\n**목표**: accuracy >= 67.1% (base_sft 이상)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 타임라인\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nDay 0 (즉시):\\\\\\\\n  EVAL: E11 SFT 시작 (2-4시간)\\\\\\\\n  TRAIN_B + E8: 환경 셋업 + dry run 준비\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nDay 0.5:\\\\\\\\n  E11 완료 → checkpoint 배포\\\\\\\\n  EVAL: E11 eval (3시간)\\\\\\\\n  TRAIN_B: E12 RL 시작\\\\\\\\n  E8: E13 RL 시작\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nDay 1-3:\\\\\\\\n  TRAIN_B: E12 RL (800 steps)\\\\\\\\n  E8: E13 RL (800 steps)\\\\\\\\n  EVAL: E12/E13 중간 checkpoint eval\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nDay 3:\\\\\\\\n  E12/E13 eval → 비교 분석\\\\\\\\n  → E14 설계 (entropy-aware, logprobs 기반)\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## 비교 평가 (Step 4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| 모델 | 설명 | 검증 메트릭 |\\\\\\\\n|---|---|---|\\\\\\\\n| base_sft | (source: control_v5_eval_readout_2026_04_04.md)\\nresults/study_2026_04_04/metacot_control_v5_study.md:304:- AIME accuracy: base_sft 13.3 vs. E8 10.0 (best meta), E3/E5/verify_sft 3.3 (worst).\\nresults/study_2026_04_04/metacot_control_v5_study.md:313:**Verify (partial).** E9 achieves 90.0 on GSM8K (vs. 80.0 base_sft), indicating that verify rewards can improve self-checking on arithmetic problems. However, E9's Wrong_Hi remains at 50.9, meaning that when the model is wrong, it is often confidently wrong. Verify is decorative in the sense that it increases verification frequency without increasing verification quality.\\nresults/study_2026_04_04/metacot_control_v5_study.md:315:**Overconfidence suppression (supported).** E8 reduces Wrong_Hi from n/a (base_sft has no confidence) to 3.6, meeting its verification criterion. However, E8 achieves this partly by reducing confidence coverage to 23.3%, so the low Wrong_Hi reflects selective confidence emission as much as improved calibration.\\nresults/study_2026_04_04/metacot_control_v5_study.md:317:**Redirect and diagnosis (not supported).** E10 was designed to train the full controller stack. 정확도를 달성하는가?\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:114:H1: Meta-CoT는 verify/redirect를 통한 오답 교정으로 base_sft를 초과할 것이다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:128:| base_sft | Plain SFT | 88.8% | 48.8% | 10.0% | 67.1% | baseline |\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:139:H1은 기각된다. 8개 Meta-CoT 모델 전체가 base_sft(67.1%)보다 -6.4pp에서 -13.2pp 낮은 정확도를 보인다. GSM8K에서의 하락이 특히 일관적이며(88.8% -> 71.8~81.4%), MATH-500에서도 모든 모델이 base_sft(48.8%)를 하회한다. AIME에서는 base_sft(10.0%)와 동일하거나 낮은 수준이다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:170:Format 버그는 SFT 모델에 체계적 불이익을 주었다. RL 모델은 GRPO 학습 과정에서 correctness reward를 받기 위해 평가 함수가 인식하는 포맷으로 수렴한 반면, SFT 모델은 학습 데이터의 포맷을 그대로 재현했다. 이 발견은 두 가지를 시사한다. 첫째, 평가 파이프라인의 답안 파싱이 모델 비교의 공정성에 직접 영향을 미치므로, 포맷 정규화는 실험 설계의 필수 요소다. 둘째, format 수정 후에도 모든 Meta-CoT 모델이 base_sft를 하회하므로, 정확도 격차의 주원인은 포맷이 아니라 다른 곳에 있다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:186:Meta-CoT 모델의 응답을 meta 존재(present) / 부재(absent) 그룹으로 나누고, 각 그룹의 문제를 base_sft로 풀었을 때의 정확도를 비교한다. base_sft 정확도가 낮은 문제는 난이도가 높은 문제다. 두 그룹 간 base 정확도 격차는 선택 편향의 크기를 나타낸다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:192:**Table 2. 선택 편향 분석: meta 존재 문제와 부재 문제의 base_sft 정확도 비교**\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:202:H5는 기각된다. 모든 모델에서 meta가 존재하는 문제의 base_sft 정확도가 현저히 낮다. E8의 경우 격차가 -37.7pp에 달한다. 이는 모델이 어려운 문제에서 선택적으로 meta를 생성함을 의미한다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:298:| 지표 | base_sft | all_sft | 변화 |\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:308:| base_sft | 1 |\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:314:H4는 기각된다. Meta 블록은 completion 토큰의 15-25%를 소비하며, 이는 동일한 토큰 예산 내에서 본문 추론에 사용할 수 있는 토큰을 줄인다. E9c와 redirect_sft에서 각각 20건, 17건의 절단이 발생했다. 이는 base_sft의 1건에 비해 현저히 높다. 절단된 응답은 최종 답을 생성하지 못하므로 반드시 오답으로 처리된다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:316:토큰 오버헤드의 영향은 문제 난이도에 따라 비대칭적이다. GSM8K와 같은 쉬운 문제에서는 base_sft도 200-300 토큰으로 풀 수 있으므로 meta 오버헤드가 절단을 유발하지 않는다. 그러나 AIME이나 MATH-500의 어려운 문제에서는 풀이 자체가 2,000-3,000 토큰을 요구하므로, meta 블록의 추가 500-1,000 토큰이 절단을 유발할 수 있다. 역설적으로, meta가 가장 필요한 어려운 문제에서 meta의 토큰 비용이 가장 큰 부담이 된다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:340:(1) 정확도: 모든 RL 모델이 base_sft를 하회한다(Table 1). E9c가 -6.4pp로 가장 양호하고, E10이 -13.2pp로 가장 큰 하락을 보인다.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:485:**결과** (base_sft, MATH500 첫 100문제):\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:496:E6 eval이 680/1030 시점에서 running accuracy 66.3%. base_sft의 전체 67.1%에 근접. 이전 \\\"probe collapse 경보\\\"(200/1030에서 75.5%)는 GSM8K 구간 편향으로, MATH500 진입 후 base_sft와 유사한 수준으로 수렴 중.\\nresults/study_metacot_v5_2026_04_05.md:542:| 1 | HO=100.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nSo\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nHC=\\\\\\\\sqrt{100^2+80^2}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{10000+6400}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{16400}\\\\n=4\\\\\\\\sqrt{1025}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nSimilarly,\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nHD=\\\\\\\\sqrt{100^2+60^2}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{10000+3600}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{13600}\\\\n=4\\\\\\\\sqrt{10625}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nBut root.\\\\n\\\\n<|meta|>\\\\nconfidence: 0.34\\\\nThe direct algebraic route is weak because it treats the three radicals separately without revealing the geometric structure that controls the minimum.\\\\nstudy_need: geometric interpretation over symbolic expansion\\\\nI should stop pushing algebra and switch to a distance-based geometric argument.\\\\n<|/meta|>\\\\n\\\\nNow interpret each term as a distance.\\\\n\\\\nLet\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nP=(x,y),\\\\\\\\qquad Q=(80,100).\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nThen\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nPQ^2=(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nExpanding,\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nPQ^2=x^2+y^2-80x-100y+4100.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nSo\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nC=PQ.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nAlso, if we place\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nA=(0,0),\\\\\\\\qquad B=(0,100),\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nthen\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{x^2+400}\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nis the distance from \\\\\\\\(A\\\\\\\\) to \\\\\\\\((x,20)\\\\\\\\), and similarly the second term is the distance from \\\\\\\\((0,20)\\\\\\\\) to \\\\\\\\(B\\\\\\\\).\\\\n\\\\nBut there is a much cleaner route.\\\\n\\\\nConsider the point\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nT=(x,y).\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nThen\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nC=PT,\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nwhere\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nP=(80,100).\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nNow look at the first two radicals:\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{x^2+400},\\\\\\\\qquad \\\\\\\\sqrt{y^2+900}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nThese suggest distances from\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n(0,0)\\\\\\\\quad\\\\\\\\text{to}\\\\\\\\quad (x,20),\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nand from\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n(0,20)\\\\\\\\quad\\\\\\\\text{to}\\\\\\\\quad (x,y).\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nA better geometric idea is to compare the whole expression to a straight-line distance.\\\\n\\\\nLet\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nU=(x,20),\\\\\\\\qquad V=(0,y).\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nThen\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{x^2+400}=\\\\\\\\sqrt{x^2+20^2}}=AU,\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nwhere \\\\\\\\(A=(0,0)\\\\\\\\).\\\\n\\\\nAlso,\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{y^2+900}}=\\\\\\\\sqrt{y^2+30^2}}=BV,\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nwhere \\\\\\\\(B=(0,30)\\\\\\\\).\\\\n\\\\nAnd\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\nC=\\\\\\\\sqrt{(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nNow use Minkowski:\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{x^2+20^2}}+\\\\\\\\sqrt{y^2+30^2}}+\\\\\\\\sqrt{(x-80)^2+(y-100)^2}}\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nis at least the straight-line distance from\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n(0,0)\\\\\\\\quad\\\\\\\\text{to}\\\\\\\\quad (80,100)\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nvia the points \\\\\\\\((x,20)\\\\\\\\), \\\\\\\\((0,y)\\\\\\\\), etc.\\\\n\\\\nThat distance is\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{80^2+100^2}}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{6400+10000}}\\\\n=\\\\\\\\sqrt{16400}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nSince \\\\\\\\(16400=4\\\\\\\\cdot 4100\\\\\\\\),\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\sqrt{16400}}=2\\\\\\\\sqrt{4100}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nSo the minimum possible value is\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\boxed{2\\\\\\\\sqrt{4100}}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\n\\\\nThis is achieved when the three displacement vectors point in the same overall direction, i.e. when\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\frac{x}{20}}=\\\\\\\\frac{x}{20}},\\\\\\\\qquad\\\\n\\\\\\\\frac{y}{30}}=\\\\\\\\frac{y}{30}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]\\\\nMore concretely, equality in Minkowski occurs when the corresponding vectors are proportional. In this geometric setting, that means the point \\\\\\\\((x,y)\\\\\\\\) lies on the line segment joining \\\\\\\\((0,0)\\\\\\\\) and \\\\\\\\((80,100)\\\\\\\\), and specifically the proportional point determined by the same factor in both coordinates.\\\\n\\\\nThus the minimum value is indeed\\\\n\\\\\\\\[\\\\n\\\\\\\\boxed{2\\\\\\\\sqrt{4100}}}.\\\\n\\\\\\\\]<|im_end|>\\\",\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_base_sft.json:2:  \\\"model\\\": \\\"1030_base_sft\\\",\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_base_sft.json:4:    \\\"model\\\": \\\"1030_base_sft\\\",\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_base_sft.json:5:    \\\"model_path\\\": \\\"checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\\",\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_base_sft.json:18:    \\\"tokenizer_name_or_path\\\": \\\"checkpoints/qwen3_base_sft\\\"\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_E5.json:22231:      \\\"answer_extracted\\\": \\\"\\\",\\nresults/eval_1030_v5/eval_1030_E5.json:22242:      \\\"first_completion\\\": \\\"<think>\\\\n\\\\n</think>\\\\n\\\\nLet \\\\\\\\(f(n)\\\\\\\\) be the state of a position with \\\\\\\\(n\\\\\\\\) tokens:\\\\n\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(f(n)=\\\\\\\\text{Win}\\\\\\\\) if the current player can force a win.\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(f(n)=\\\\\\\\text{Lose}\\\\\\\\) if the current player will lose if the opponent plays optimally.\\\\n\\\\nSince the moves are only \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) or \\\\\\\\(4\\\\\\\\), we can compute the state recursively:\\\\n\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(f(0)=\\\\\\\\text{Lose}\\\\\\\\), since the current player removes the last token and therefore wins; but in the recursive convention here, \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\) is the terminal position after the last move, so that position is won by the player who just moved. To make the losing state at \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\) consistent with the game rule, we should define the state after a move, and the losing positions turn out to be the numbers in the losing chain starting from \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\). So let us just compute the losing positions directly from the recursion below.\\\\n\\\\nFor \\\\\\\\(n>0\\\\\\\\):\\\\n\\\\n- if the current player can move to a losing position for the opponent, then the current position is winning;\\\\n- if every legal move leads to a winning position for the opponent, then the current position is losing.\\\\n\\\\nSo we compute the losing positions directly:\\\\n\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\) is losing.\\\\n- From \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\), you can only move to \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\), so \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) is winning.\\\\n- From \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\), all moves go to winning positions \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) or \\\\\\\\(-2)\\\\\\\\)? Better to compute carefully:\\\\n\\\\nActually let us do it cleanly with the losing positions explicitly listed in the winning/losing chain:\\\\n\\\\nIf \\\\\\\\(n\\\\\\\\) is losing, then from every legal move \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) or \\\\\\\\(4\\\\\\\\), the resulting position is winning.\\\\n\\\\nWe compute the losing positions by backward induction:\\\\n\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\) is losing.\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\): can move to \\\\\\\\(0\\\\\\\\), so winning.\\\\n- \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\): moves to \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) or \\\\\\\\(-2)\\\\\\\\), but negative positions are not part of the actual game, so we should only consider \\\\\\\\(n\\\\\\\\ge 0\\\\\\\\). Thus the only relevant move from \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\) is to \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\), so \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\) is losing? That cannot be right because from \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\), removing \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\) leaves \\\\\\\\(1\\\\\\\\), which is winning for the opponent, so \\\\\\\\(2\\\\\\\\) is losing. Good.\\\\n\\\\nSo we continue this losing-chain computation. -"""Meta-CoT GRPO v2: Full FT + GDPO + modular rewards.
+"""Meta-CoT GRPO v2: full FT + GDPO + decomposed control rewards.
 
 Key design:
   - Full fine-tuning (NO LoRA)
   - GDPO monkey-patch: per-reward normalization before summing
-  - 4 experiments via --mode: E1, E2, E3, E4
-  - Response samples saved every 50 steps
-  - Token entropy logged to wandb
+  - Reward ablations via --mode: E1-E10, including E9/E9b/E9c decompositions
+  - Calibration and confidence-revision rewards remain active before full control rewards
+  - Probe-aligned local-calibration experiments are isolated in E6/E7
+  - Explicit control behaviors are decomposed into verify-only / redirect-only /
+    diagnosis-only runs before the full controller E10
+  - Response samples and reward logs are saved for later qualitative analysis
 
 Usage:
